steps.title: Understand what margin of error means
steps.content: A margin of error defines the interval around a sample statistic where the true population parameter is expected to fall. We construct the confidence interval by adding and subtracting the margin from the point estimate.

steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval
steps.content:
With point estimate 38% and margin of error 2.76%, the confidence interval is:

Lower bound: $38\% - 2.76\% = 35.24\%$

Upper bound: $38\% + 2.76\% = 40.76\%$

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content:
More than $40.76\%$ of all adults who own tablets use their tablets to read e-books. claims more than 40.76%, exceeding our upper bound.

Two other options incorrectly reject all conclusions, misunderstanding that a random sample provides valid population estimates.

Between $35.24\%$ and $40.76\%$ of all adults who own tablets use their tablets to read e-books. states the interval between 35.24% and 40.76%, which matches our confidence interval exactly.

That matches the correct answer - B is correct.
